channel.title: JLT Spotlight (LinkedIn)
channel.description: Weekly Cruise, Hotel & Destination spotlights from JLT Group

item.title: The outer Seychelles are some of the most inaccessible waters on earth.
item.description:
The outer Seychelles are some of the most inaccessible waters on earth. That inaccessibility is exactly what makes them relevant right now for agents working with ultra-luxury and expedition clients.
Aqua Expeditions launches Aqua Lares in February 2026: a 77-metre superyacht with an icebreaker hull, 30 guests, 32 crew, sailing to territory almost no other vessel reaches. Aldabra Atoll, a UNESCO World Heritage Site home to over 100,000 giant tortoises, is the centrepiece of the 9-night itinerary.
Shorter inner-island options run from 5 nights, with extensions to Zanzibar and Tanzania on the 10- and 11-night sailings. Rates start from £7,100 per person. Individual cabins are bookable now, not charter-only.
This is an early-access window. Agents working with ultra-luxury and expedition clients should have it on the radar. Trade enquiries go through Exsus Travel.

item.title: In JLT, we track supplier rates for what they tell agents about a booking opportunity, not only for their headline number.
item.description:
In JLT, we track supplier rates for what they tell agents about a booking opportunity, not only for their headline number.
The B2B rate currently showing for X2 Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit, a 4-star BHMA Hotel Group property in Bangkok's Onnut area, is approximately £21 per person per night for November 2026, and it is fully refundable. That last detail matters.
November is a long way out. A refundable rate means agents can secure a well-designed, well-connected Bangkok base now, hold the price, and build the wider itinerary around it without asking clients to commit non-refundable money to plans that may move.
BTS Skytrain on the doorstep. Strong fit for Thailand first-timers and couples doing Bangkok before the islands. Worth adding to the knowledge bank now.

item.title: Paros is one destination we are watching closely for 2026.
item.description:
Paros is one destination we are watching closely for 2026. As Santorini continues to command premium prices and peak-season capacity constraints, agents are finding a stronger value case one ferry-ride over in the Cyclades.
Paros offers the whitewashed villages, blue domes and turquoise water clients picture when they ask for Santorini, alongside proper sandy beaches the cliffside island cannot match. Kolympithres is the standout: granite boulders worn into natural swimming pools. Direct flights operate into Paros National Airport, and onward connections to Naxos, Antiparos and Milos are fast.
Shoulder season 2026 is the window to act on. May, June and September offer full swimming conditions with pricing and availability at their most favourable. Agents building Cyclades itineraries for next year should have Paros on the table now.

item.title: A first-hand trip report from a Regent Seven Seas specialist is in front of our agents this week.
item.description:
A first-hand trip report from a Regent Seven Seas specialist is in front of our agents this week.
Harriet Smith sailed Seven Seas Splendor Lisbon to Monte Carlo over seven nights and filed a port-by-port account of how the ship feels to be on. The thread through the whole report: space.
No queuing, no crowded pool, no competition for a table. Embarkation in Lisbon ran to under ten minutes. For an experienced cruiser weighing up a trade, that answers the reservation directly.
Two sailings are bookable now: a 14-night Lisbon to Rome Mediterranean departure on 17 October 2027 and an 11-night Canada and New England foliage sailing on 17 September 2026. The September 2026 window is close enough that agents with a ready client should move this week. On an all-suite ship, suite choice is the first conversation, and booking early protects it.
Within JLT, first-hand specialist reports are what we put in front of agents. Real detail converts enquiries.

item.title: Most Algarve enquiries default to the coastal strip. Viceroy at Ombria gives agents something deliberately different.
item.description:
Most Algarve enquiries default to the coastal strip. Viceroy at Ombria gives agents something deliberately different.
The property sits near Querenca, built across a 150-hectare estate designed as a traditional Portuguese hill village. All 151 suites include a kitchen or kitchenette, a living room and a private terrace. An award-winning GEO-Certified golf course, a dark-sky stargazing programme in a certified reserve and a dedicated spa make this a strong fit across golf, wellness and multigenerational travel.
In JLT, we track properties that give agents a clear story to lead with rather than a rate to defend. Viceroy at Ombria is that kind of property.
A live Exsus offer runs until 30 July 2026: seven nights from £1,095pp, one complimentary night included, £100pp saved. Agents with Algarve enquiries in play should have it in the toolkit.

item.title: Chongqing is not on most agents' China lists yet. Within JLT, we think it should be.
item.description:
Chongqing is not on most agents' China lists yet. Within JLT, we think it should be.
The city is a river megacity with a striking futuristic skyline and a centuries-old wellness tradition rooted in hot springs, qigong and traditional Chinese medicine. That combination of cultural depth and contemporary energy is exactly what a post-classic-circuit China client is looking for.
The bookable peg for this moment is Wendy Wu Tours' China Wellness & Wisdom Retreat: eight days from £3,990pp, with exclusive access to a qigong master, reflexology and a Miao Medicine Clinic visit. One departure, 9 May 2027.
For agents with wellness clients or travellers who have already covered Beijing, Shanghai and Xi'an, this is a destination worth raising now. Single-departure products reward the early conversation. Agents who have those conversations this week are better placed than those who wait.

item.title: Arizona has been underplayed in UK agents' USA conversations for years, sitting in the shadow of California and Nevada on Southwest itineraries. In JLT, that is starting to change, and the timing behind it is concrete.
item.description:
Arizona has been underplayed in UK agents' USA conversations for years, sitting in the shadow of California and Nevada on Southwest itineraries. In JLT, that is starting to change, and the timing behind it is concrete.
A stronger pound against the dollar, a daily British Airways service from Heathrow into Phoenix, and a state that covers luxury resort, adventure, wellness and multi-gen family in a single itinerary loop. Scottsdale gives agents a genuine luxury case.
Sedona and the Grand Canyon deliver the visual anchor clients will want to talk about. Tucson's guest ranches are the family alternative to Florida that many agents have not put on their pitch deck yet.
Visit Arizona is running a free live webinar for agents this Tuesday 7 July at 11am BST, with a £50 shopping voucher prize draw on the call. The autumn booking window is open now.
Agents building October to February USA itineraries should have Arizona in the mix. The full spotlight is on the blog.
Blog link to be added in comments after 24-48 hrs.

item.title: Gleneagles is back on the JLT radar as we head into the autumn booking window.
item.description:
Gleneagles is back on the JLT radar as we head into the autumn booking window.
The 850-acre Perthshire estate holds three championship golf courses, including the PGA Centenary, host of the 2014 Ryder Cup and 2019 Solheim Cup. Restaurant Andrew Fairlie carries two Michelin stars and is the only two-star restaurant in Scotland at publication.
Salmon fishing on the Tay, a gundog school, a riding school, and a spa with wellness residencies mean the estate holds an entire mixed-interest party without anyone leaving the grounds.
For agents, the opportunity is the multi-day sporting itinerary. A three or four-night programme with golf, fishing, spa and a Michelin dinner is a materially different product from a single room night, and it is the structure that earns margin and demonstrates visible value.
Agents with HNW, sporting, luxury family, or milestone clients circling September or October should have Gleneagles on the table now, while autumn availability remains.
Blog link to be added in comments after 24-48 hrs.

item.title: Aurora Expeditions at 35: a campaign worth moving on before September
item.description:
Aurora Expeditions at 35: a campaign worth moving on before September
Aurora Expeditions has launched its 35th-year campaign, running 1 July to 30 September 2026. It offers up to 30% off plus up to £2,600 air credit on Antarctica 26/27 and Arctic 27 sailings, applied as an additional voyage discount. Alongside it, the "100 Reasons to Sell AE Expeditions" UK trade incentive runs in the same window.
Agents holding Antarctica enquiries have an unusually clean commercial window to convert them within this three-month campaign.
The Greg Mortimer and Sylvia Earle use X-Bow hull technology, cutting through the Drake Passage rather than riding over it. At 130 to 160 guests, they deliver Zodiac time, ice camping, and citizen-science programming that larger vessels cannot match.
The client profile is adventure-luxury travellers, wildlife-first guests, bucket-list retirees, and second-cruisers ready for the next step in polar travel. The air credit removes the objection that most often stops this client committing.
Agents with expedition enquiries on file: pull those names this week and match each to a voyage before September closes.
Blog link to be added in comments after 24-48 hrs.

item.title: In JLT, we track destination news when the access story changes in a way that opens up new bookings. The TUI direct flight to Zanzibar is one of those moments.
item.description: In JLT, we track destination news when the access story changes in a way that opens up new bookings. The TUI direct flight to Zanzibar is one of those moments. From 3 November 2027, TUI will operate the only nonstop UK service between London Gatwick and Zanzibar, twice weekly, through to 22 March 2028. Winter 2027 packages go on sale 2 July 2026. Lead-in prices start from £1,615 per person (two adults sharing, all-inclusive, seven nights including direct flights and transfers). Eight hotels on the island, plus the option to build in a short safari connection to Nyerere or the Serengeti via TUI Musement. For agents with clients comparing Indian Ocean options on value, Zanzibar now has the route to match the product. Note the 2 July on-sale date and build your shortlist before the window opens.

item.title: This week's Hotel Spotlight is Bodrum Loft, and it solves a specific client brief.
item.description: This week's Hotel Spotlight is Bodrum Loft, and it solves a specific client brief. The hotel is a cluster of white minimalist villas stepping down a pine-forested hillside to a private cove in Demirbükü Bay, about 40 minutes from Bodrum airport. Nearly every room has a sea view, and the mood is calm rather than loud… closer to a private beach club than a party resort. For agents, that is the value. It matches the client who wants Bodrum's design and service without the crowds, with a strong table in Paper Moon and villa kitchens that suit families and longer stays. Hold it for the next client who asks for luxury without the scene.

item.title: This week's Spotlight is Silversea, and the timing is the story.
item.description: This week's Spotlight is Silversea, and the timing is the story. The line is offering up to 45 per cent off select voyages across all seven continents, with suites to be reserved by 30 June 2026. That two-week window is what makes this worth acting on now, not the headline number. For agents working in the luxury tier, Silversea's all-suite, all-inclusive model covers far more than the cabin, and reduced deposits from 15 per cent make securing a suite an easier message for clients with a voyage in mind. Three autumn 2026 sailings worth flagging: Silver Ray (Balearics, from £3,550 pp), Silver Nova and Silver Spirit (both Greek Isles and Istanbul, from £3,750 and £3,850 pp). Two overlapping Greek Isles departures give agents useful comparison material when a client is weighing schedule over ship. Pull your luxury cruise list before 30 June.
